title: Chapter 341
content:
Chapter 341: Chapter 341 You’re Mine Now (R-18)

Dickson’s eyes narrowed as he caught sight of the two black cars slowing down ahead. He immediately hit the brakes, letting the SUV come to a steady halt behind them. For a few seconds, silence sat heavy inside the vehicle, only the low hum of the engine breaking it. He tilted his head toward Vanessa and Kelly, his tone flat but edged with concern.

"You two sure you wanna go inside alone with those guys?" he asked, glancing between them. His voice carried the kind of weight that made it clear he already thought this was a bad idea.

Vanessa reached into her shirt and adjusted the small mic she had hidden against her skin. "If anything goes wrong, I’ll just teleport us out instantly," she replied with confidence, though her eyes betrayed a flicker of unease. She tucked the mic neatly under her collar, making sure it was in place.

Kelly didn’t waste time. She gave a sharp nod, her hand already on the door handle. "We don’t have a choice," she said quietly before pushing the door open. As soon as she stepped out, the morning air hit her face. Her eyes instantly flicked down the long road in front of the house.

Far in the distance, she caught sight of Lilith’s sleek black car. It was parked deliberately far away, positioned where no one would notice it easily. Kelly knew the strategy—Lilith had placed herself where she could swoop in the second things turned south. Backup was always a breath away, though it didn’t make the walk ahead any lighter.

Vanessa climbed out next. She adjusted her top slightly, conscious of the hidden wire taped against her skin. The faint buzz of the comm let her know it was live—Lilith, Seo Yeon, Dickson, and even Liam could hear everything. Well, maybe not Liam. He was probably too busy screwing Ann right now to even bother listening.

Far away in the black car, Lilith sat in silence. Her sharp golden eyes never left the compound. She leaned forward, tapping the side of her earpiece. "Stormzy is leaving his car. The girls should follow him now to avoid suspicion," she said quietly.

Her tone was calm but it carried a firmness that made Seo Yeon, sitting stiffly beside her, swallow nervously.

Since Vanessa and Kelly weren’t directly patched in, Dickson had to play the middleman. He shifted in his seat and muttered into the comm. "Lilith says follow Stormzy now. Don’t stall."

Both women gave a subtle nod. Kelly rolled her shoulders, bracing herself, and Vanessa smoothed her shirt once before moving.

Stormzy’s heavy door swung open, and he stepped out with the kind of swagger that made it obvious he thought he owned the ground he walked on. His four guards immediately flanked him, standing tall and alert. His eyes, sharp and hostile, swept toward Kelly and Vanessa. He sneered slightly before speaking.

"Your driver better not fuck around in this region," Stormzy said coldly, his voice thick with warning. "If he’s found sniffing around, we won’t hesitate to blow his fucking brains out."

Kelly and Vanessa instinctively looked back toward the SUV. Their eyes locked on Dickson, and though they didn’t say a word, the look was enough. The message was loud and clear: don’t be stupid.

Inside the SUV, Dickson leaned back in his seat, pulling his phone lazily from his pocket. He wasn’t intimidated. In fact, he grinned faintly as he unlocked it and began scrolling through his social media feed. "Damn, look at that ass," he muttered under his breath with a smirk, shaking his head at whatever he was seeing on X.

Meanwhile, Kelly and Vanessa fell into step behind Stormzy. The guards didn’t give them space, staying close on either side as if daring them to make the wrong move. Their boots crunched against the gravel as they were led toward the compound.

The property was massive. A stretch of green land expanded around them, dotted with towering trees that gave the place a deceptive tranquility. But the presence of guards everywhere shattered any illusion of peace. Men with rifles were stationed at posts, their eyes cold and unblinking.

As they approached the first checkpoint, the tall metal gate came into view. It was painted a deep, dull black, the kind that radiated wealth but also secrecy. A guard stood at attention, his hand already hovering near a control panel.

The moment his eyes caught Stormzy, he didn’t hesitate. He pressed the button, and with a heavy mechanical groan, the gate began to slide open.

Kelly glanced at Vanessa, and though they didn’t speak, the tension in both their shoulders told the same story. They were walking into a den where one wrong word could mean death.

The gate finished opening, the path inside stretching wide, with the house still visible in the distance.
